evals.prompt: Create a shell script that sets up a git worktree workflow using a bare repository. The script should clone a repo as bare, create worktrees for main and develop branches in sibling directories, and include helper functions for adding new feature branch worktrees and cleaning up merged ones.
evals.expected_output: Shell script for git worktree workflow setup
expectations: Uses git worktree add to create worktrees
Creates worktrees in organized directory structure
Includes function to add new worktrees for feature branches
Includes cleanup function for removing merged worktrees
Handles the main/master and develop branches

evals.prompt: Write a guide with commands for using git worktrees to work on a hotfix while keeping feature development separate. Show how to create worktrees from an existing repo, switch between them, share dependencies (like node_modules via symlinks), and properly clean up worktrees when done.
evals.expected_output: Commands and explanation for git worktree parallel development
expectations: Shows git worktree add command with branch specification
Demonstrates creating worktree from existing (non-bare) repository
Includes strategy for sharing dependencies (symlinks or similar)
Shows git worktree remove or prune for cleanup
Addresses the hotfix vs feature branch parallel workflow
